parsed.answer: In religious and folk traditions, an exorcist is believed to have the ability to expel demons, evil spirits, or malevolent entities from a person or place through prayer, ritual, and invocation of divine authority. In Roman Catholic tradition this is a formal rite. In reality, there is no scientific evidence that exorcists possess supernatural abilities; what they may actually provide is psychological comfort, a placebo effect, or address underlying mental health issues through belief-based ritual.
